raw_input:
[5.0/5] An excellent shave cream... Went to T&H on St James St this year and had a straight razor shave for the first time. Fantastic! Loved the T&H shave cream and decided to buy some. I decided on the Lavender for something different. Thsi product when used with a brush and mug produces an exceptional amount of thick lather that lubricates my face very well. It cleans up nicely and moisturizes but does not leave any residue. I have bought sampler packs of Musgo real, TOBS, Geo Trumper etc and although each one of these are good (expecially the TOBS avocado and Eton College for the price) IMO the T&H is the best. I bought a jar of Dreanought due to reviews but it dies not lubricate as well for me at the T&H. Also, the Dreadnoght tub was not filled with cream to the brim (a sizeable void was in the tub) whereas the T&H tub is jammed packed and only requires an almond sized amount for a 3 pass shave. Fantastic stuff. Will try the other scents at some point and expect hem to be as good. [5.0/5] Great Product This product meet my expectations. I highly recommend this product. It has a very pleasant fragrance and one gets a smooth shave. [1.0/5] Thought $130.00 would hve been for an entire year. Canceled the order and went with several products at 15% the cost. Not anywhere worth the money for me to use. [5.0/5] Five Stars Much better than Castle Forbes, though CF pure oils, chemical scent is fine [5.0/5] The best shaving cream I've used!!! I was a big fan of Taylor of Old Bond St due to it's great smell, reasonable price, and comfortable shave. I began to have issues with irritation after a few months use, and that's when I decided to switch to a more notable brand. I am completely shocked at how little shave cream you need and how smooth a shave I get. I use a DE razor with (Feather Hi-stainless blades) and a badger hair brush for every shave. You will not be disappointed with how silky smooth this shaving cream is. I am speaking conservatively when I say this is the the Rolls Royce of shaving cream. I highly recommend this shaving cream, and if you are contemplating the price....since when have you purchased a new Rolls Royce for Chevrolet money? [5.0/5] Best out there One to my second jar now. I've been using it over year now. It lasts more than I expected. Leaves your skin smooth after a close shave [5.0/5] Fantastic smell on this superior shaving product! Truefitt & Hill make great shaving products. Their products are a little on the expensive side - at least at first glance, but a little of the shaving products go a long way. I have been sampling shaving balms, and shaving creams & soaps for the past several years, and so far, the Trufitt & Hill products are superior to the rest of the field. So far as I can tell, the Lavender Shaving Cream is not sold in a series of products (balms, soaps, shaving cream etc) but just the shaving cream itself. That is a shame, because I REALLY like the smell of this cream, and the lather it produces is magnificent! I use a high quality badger shaving brush and apply the cream directly to my (wet) face for lathering. It takes just a dab to produce a luxurious lather that easily lasts the four passes I normally take with my double edged razor. The lather that is produced keeps my face very moist during the process of shaving; the razor glides effortlessly and a very comfortable shave is obtained. I highly recommend this product! [5.0/5] Nice cream I like this cream. If you have a good brush, you need only traces of this cream to put onto the brush - very economic. Smell is nice and not too strong. Very good feeling after shaving. I also use after-shaving balm from the same company. [5.0/5] Smoothest shave I have been using this cream for years. It's given me the smoothest shave ever. I don't have heavy facial hair so one tub will last me at least a year. [5.0/5] A must have I try on occasion to find something else
but without fail I keep coming back.
I give up, just going to not wander again [5.0/5] Brilliant Shaving Cream Truefitt & Hill Lavender is such a joy to use, it is downright therapeutic. A little bit goes a long way, and it's easy to create a nice lather. It feels very smooth and soothing. I get a close shave with little irritation. My skin feels better hydrated after using this than with most shaving creams.

Fragrance: The primary note is lavender, as you might have guessed. This lavender is masculine, and there is a bit of a "barbershop" smell. The effect is very relaxing and calming. This is not the sharp, pungent, synthetic lavender that you get with many cheap men's fragrances and grooming products. It is cool, natural smelling, and "round".

So far, this is the best cream I've used. It is also the most expensive, but well worth it. Other good choices are Proraso (not as good, but great price) and Taylor of Old Bond Street (close to the same shave quality, cheaper, smells decent). I have not tried Geo F. Trumper yet, so can't offer a comparison to that. [4.0/5] Excellent Truefitt & Hill Product Being a huge fan of Truefitt & Hill shaving cream jars but new to the lavender scent, this product smells and shaves great. Delivery via Amazon was also prompt. [5.0/5] Good stuff This is some of the best cream for ensuring a very close shave allowing the face to withstand the rigors of daily shaving. [4.0/5] Close shave I originally received a tub of this as a gift. After using it realized it gave the best, smoothest shave I've ever had. I have tried other Truefitt & Hill shave creams but none worked as well. Besides, it smells pretty good too. [5.0/5] One of my standards. T&H, Trumper, Castle & Forbes, Taylor...line a few jars up for a shaving rotation...they're all good. I have my personal preferences, you will, too, but they're all good. A good shaving cream, a fine brush, a good blade...very subjective on which one an individual will prefer...and even preferences will undergo changes.
Make shaving enjoyable. Buy a "real" razor, learn to make scraping hair fun. [5.0/5] How much should the best cost? In short, this is THE best shaving cream/soap/gel/foam I have ever had the joy of using. Period.

Here is the situation: I have a really thick, course, wiry beard combined with ultra sensitive skin. Quite possibly the worst combination of factors when it comes time to shave. The result has been a life-long (25+ years of shaving) quest for the best combination of products, hoping to produce a smooth shave that doesn't leave leave my face resembling a slab of meat. I have tried practically every method and product known to man, short of ritual sacrifice. Some products worked well, some were abysmal, but I always wondered if there was "something better" and would eventually move on from the latest greatest. Then I found this product about a year ago. I am finished looking for a better cream/soap/gel/foam.

Metrics: I shave in the shower, after I have washed my face. I use a safety razor with Feather blades. I sometimes use shaving oil, but only if I have more than 4 days of growth. The cream is applied using a super badger brush.

First, the scent of this product only lasts about 10 minutes after shaving, so it isn't too perfumicatious. The lather this produces is unbelievable. My previous benchmarks were Proraso and the Art of Shaving, both great products that did the job. This stuff is in a completely different realm. You don't so much work the cream into the brush as much as just wave the shaving brush over the top of the tub once or twice, gathering up shaving cream fumes. That is all you need to produce more than enough lather. I suspect backward engineered alien technology is in play here.

The quality of the resulting shave is beyond belief. Nothing more to say about that.

Yeah, but what about the cost? My first round of Trufitt & Hill lasted about a year. No kidding. A can of average foamy at your local store comes to about $3.50. If that can lasts you 6 weeks, you will use about 8 of those in a year, equalling $28. $28 for a year of horrible shaves, or spend about that same amount for a year of baby bottom skin with no irritation. The choice is yours. [5.0/5] Makes shaving a joy A must have! Smells great, makes tons of lather, makes skin soft, prevents nicks due to drag. It's perfect ! [5.0/5] Shaving Cream for True Aficionados I love my morning shave - it's a ritual for me. Dunking my razor and brush in warm water every morning, working up a good lather, and shaving is such a blissful experience. If you are serious about your skin, but a good shaving cream. Trufitt and Hill is one of the best. I did a lot of research before I bought it. It actually gives you a very close shave. The aroma is deep and masculine.
PS: It lasted me 6 months and I shave everyday. [5.0/5] Great Product This is a great product for a smooth shave and a great lather for a close shave. The fragrance is gret also.

response:
Most reviewers love this shaving cream. Say it best they ever used. Thick lather, very little needed per shave, tub last long time - many say one year or more. Skin feel smooth, moisturized, no irritation. Lavender smell masculine, natural, not sharp or fake, like barbershop. Good for sensitive skin, thick beard too.

Many compare to other premium creams - Taylor of Old Bond Street, Proraso, Castle Forbes, Geo Trumper, Art of Shaving. T&H beat all in most opinions. Price seem high but last so long, cost per shave actually low.

One reviewer cancel order, say too expensive, not worth it. Another say good but not quite Castle Forbes level.

Best used with badger brush and safety razor. Only need almond-size amount. Lather last multiple passes. Face feel hydrated after.
